question.text: Which of the following categories is NOT one of the three insurance needs categories identified in the NISM curriculum?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** The three insurance needs categories as per the NISM curriculum are: (1) Income replacement (replacing income lost due to death/disability), (2) Income protection (protecting income during temporary disability/illness), and (3) Asset protection (protecting physical assets like home, car). Wealth accumulation is an investment objective, not an insurance need. **The Trap:** Wealth accumulation sounds relevant because many insurance products (ULIPs, endowment plans) have an investment component. However, the curriculum clearly distinguishes insurance needs from investment needs. Insurance is primarily about protection, not wealth creation. **Cross-Exam Context:** Insurance needs analysis is tested in NISM Series X-A and X-B. The concept aligns with the financial planning pyramid where protection forms the base and wealth creation sits at the top. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** When conducting a needs analysis, map each insurance product to one of these three categories. This helps clients understand why they need different types of insurance and prevents over-insurance or under-insurance in any category.
